bird.name: Bicknell's thrush
bird.summary: Bicknell's thrush (Catharus bicknelli) is a medium-sized thrush, at 17.5 cm (6.9 in) and 28 g (0.99 oz). One of North America's rarest and…

locations.name: 伊斯帕尼奥拉岛

locations.name: 多明尼加
locations.summary: 多米尼加共和國（西班牙語：República Dominicana）是位於加勒比海的島國，與西側的海地共處於伊斯帕尼奥拉岛，陸地面積48,670平方公里，首都與最大都市為聖多明哥，官方語言為西班牙語，貨幣為多米尼加比索。據美國中央情報局的…

relations.subject: Bicknell's thrush
relations.evidence: The Bicknell's and gray-cheeked thrushes, along with the veery, make up a close-knit group of migrant species. The birds migrate to the Greater Antilles, with an estimated 90% of the individuals wintering in Hispaniola (mostly in the Dominican Republic, but also Haiti).{{cite journal|url=http://www.soh.org.do/Palmchatter1_2%20English.pdf |title=Bicknell's Thrush Conservation—Are Females Getting Squeezed?

relations.subject: Bicknell's thrush
relations.evidence: Scientists believe that industrial pollution is one of the main reasons for the decline of the red spruce, an important element in Bicknell's thrush habitat in the United States
relations.object_summary: 农药、工业污染与水体污染影响食物链安全。

relations.subject: Bicknell's thrush
relations.evidence: Non-native species are potential risks throughout the thrush's range; introduced moose on Cape Breton Island consume the saplings of the tree species birds breed on, and invasive brown and black rats in the Caribbean may pose a potential threat. With climate change shifting temperature up elevation on mountains, Bicknell's thrush may suffer from asymmetrical and aggressive competition from the Swainson's thrush (Catharus swainsoni). Bicknell's thrush has a higher concentration of mercury in its blood than any lower-elevation Catharus thrush. Mercury level increases greatly with altitude and its concentration bioaccumulate in the food web, probably explaining why it decreases as breeding season advances and birds begin to feed more on fruits. High mercury concentrations may cause reproductive impairment. There is also considerable concern about the degradation of Bicknell's thrush's wintering habitats
relations.object_summary: 长期改变水文节律与生态系统稳定性的全球变化因素。
